title: Gingered Thai Tofu Curry
description: A bright and tangy Thai curry made with tofu, ginger, and madras paste, perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: Press tofu for 15 minutes, then cube into bite‑sized pieces.
Heat 1 tbsp oil over medium heat and sauté tofu until golden on all sides.
Add madras paste, grated ginger, and stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in coconut milk and vegetable stock, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Stir in tamarind paste, lime juice, coconut sugar, and soy sauce, tasting and adjusting seasonings.
Add bell pepper, carrot, and green beans, covering the vegetables and letting them steam in the curry for 8 minutes.
Turn off the heat and allow the curry to rest for 3 minutes so flavors meld.
Serve hot over steamed jasmine rice and garnish with chopped cilantro and sliced green onions.
If desired, add a splash of coconut milk for a creamier texture.
Clean up the pan and enjoy a flavorful, jain‑friendly meal.
